input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>The vehicle moves forward past a stationary eight-wheeled armored military transport painted in camouflage. As the perspective shifts, the rear section becomes visible alongside green trees. A vertical white sign displaying Japanese text appears near the rear tires. Inside, passengers remain seated facing forward while the outdoor scenery pans slightly right.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

In the background, a blue and white tram displaying "UNIVERSAL STUDIOS" remains stationary near green trees and a fence, while vertical metal poles in the foreground occasionally obscure the view.

[120-124] A white hatchback with black and red decals rotates on a black hydraulic mechanical arm, shifting from a nose-down angle to a steep nose-up position before leveling into a horizontal side profile. To the left, a dark grey hatchback remains elevated on a separate arm. In the background, a blue and white tram labeled 'UNIVERSAL STUDIOS' sits stationary behind a fence, while a person with brown hair appears in the bottom right foreground near vertical metal poles. [124-128] The camera advances, keeping the white hatchback horizontally suspended. A building with a green "DRIFT BAR" sign is visible behind the vehicles. The tram moves forward past the scene. A chain-link fence slides into the foreground, partially obscuring the suspended white hatchback viewed through the metal mesh, while the dark grey car on the left becomes mostly out of frame as the view shifts completely past the stunt zone.

[128-130] The camera moves right, bringing a chain-link fence into the foreground which partially obscures a white hatchback and dark grey hatchback suspended horizontally. Behind them, a structure with "DRIFT BAR" text stands near trees while a blue tram exits frame right. [130-134] As the camera advances, a corrugated metal wall with graffiti slides in from the right, blocking the suspended cars, including the white hatchback with black graphics. A passenger with brown hair is seen in the bottom right. [134-136] The camera tilts up to the tram's white interior ceiling and silver support poles. A blue-framed monitor hangs overhead, and through the front opening, another tram vehicle is visible ahead on the track as the exterior fence disappears.

[136-138] The camera angle shifts upward to show the white interior ceiling of the tram, featuring silver support poles and a blue-framed monitor screen. Through the front opening, another white and blue tram vehicle is visible ahead on the track, while the tops of passengers' heads appear in the lower foreground. [138-144] The video transitions to a black end screen featuring "TheCoasterViews" in large red and white text at the top and large yellow "Subscribe" text at the bottom. White text on the left thanks viewers for watching videos from Universal Studios Hollywood. A small rectangular video inset in the center displays a water scene near a dock that changes to water splashing, and finally, a large fireball explosion erupts on a wooden dock structure near the water.

queries.question: What number is displayed on the black wall structure visible on the left side as the tram approaches the building?
Who was driving the white hatchback during the fire stunt? A. A professional stuntman B. A crash test dummy C. Unable to answer D. An automated remote system

answers.text: C

[2-6] As the armored vehicle exits left, vertical signs emerge near a pole, including a white banner with Japanese text, red illuminated signs, and a black sign reading 'Nappa.' The transport approaches a large beige gate structure spanning the road against green hills. [6-8] Advancing toward the gate, vertical ads pass on the left, revealing a blue sign with a white tooth icon and red signs with Japanese text. Passengers remain seated in silhouette. [8-12] Leaving the signs behind, a black rectangular structure marked 'SS' and later '55' appears on the left. A second white open-sided tour bus becomes visible on the right, running parallel near the gate entrance. A red circular sign is visible on the beige wall next to the black structure as the vehicle continues forward toward the looming gate.

recent_thinks: [12-14] The white open-sided tour bus on the right becomes fully visible, running parallel to the current vehicle. On the left, a red circular sign appears on the beige wall next to the black structure marked '55'. The large beige gate structure looms larger ahead as the vehicle continues forward.

[14-16] The vehicle moves closer to the beige gate, bringing the black structure on the left into sharper focus, now clearly reading "55 STAGE." Beside it, the red circular sign displays "The Fast and the Furious Tokyo Drift" text. The parallel white tour bus on the right remains visible alongside the road as passengers sit in silhouette.

[16-18] The vehicle approaches the black building on the left, making the white 55 STAGE text legible. Beside it, the red sign shows Fast and the Furious Tokyo Drift logo. The tour bus stays visible on the right. Passengers sit facing forward as the beige gate fills the view.

[18-20] The vehicle advances closer to the black structure labeled "55 STAGE" on the left. Adjacent to it, a large red banner becomes clearly legible, displaying the logo for "The Fast and the Furious Tokyo Drift." The tram follows the curving road, bringing the signage into sharp focus while passengers remain seated in the foreground.
